data.title: China Maintains Benchmark Lending Rates
data.description:
China on Thursday kept the benchmark lending rates unchanged, with the one-year loan prime rate at 3.1% and the five-year LPR at 3.6%, according to the People's Bank of China.

The one-year LPR is the market-based benchmark lending rate, while the five-year LPR influences the pricing of mortgages.

data.title: Fed Statement, Dot-Plot Update Are Proof of Data Volatility — Market Talk
data.description: The Federal Reserve's statement and updated dot plot underscore the data volatility and uncertainty the U.S. central bank faces in resuming its cutting cycle, says IFM Investors' ​Ryan Weldon in a note. "While the jobs market has stabilized and inflation appears to be slowly trending towards its target, the Fed will need several months of consistent data to convince them to cut their policy rate again," the investment director says. The Fed will need to balance its decision-making around its sharply reduced economic growth projections and its expectation for higher inflation in 2025, says Weldon, who is also a portfolio manager. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)

data.title: STMicroelectronics Vice Chairman Resigns
data.description:
By Adria Calatayud

STMicroelectronics said Maurizio Tamagnini, vice chairman of the company's supervisory board and former chair, is resigning from his role with immediate effect.

Tamagnini joined the European chipmaker's supervisory board in 2014 and served as chairman and vice chairman--a position he held since 2023--during his tenure, the company said late Wednesday.

Write to Adria Calatayud at adria.calatayud@wsj.com

data.title: South African Markets - Factors to watch on March 20
data.description:
The following scheduled economic events, equity and currency market moves may affect South African markets on Thursday.

ECONOMIC EVENTS

South African central bank interest rate announcement

SOUTH AFRICAN MARKETS

The South African rand was steady on Wednesday after statistics agency data showed the country's year on year inflation rate remained unchanged in February.

On the stock market, the Top-40 index closed about 1.5% higher.

GLOBAL MARKETS

Asia shares were hobbled by weakness in Chinese markets on Thursday and struggled to build on Wall Street's rally, even as investor sentiment was lifted by the prospect that the Federal Reserve could still deliver two rate cuts this year.

WALL STREET

U.S. stocks rallied on Wednesday after the Federal Reserve kept rates unchanged as widely expected, and the central bank and investors continue to gauge how President Donald Trump's tariff policies affect the economy and inflation.

GOLD

Gold rose to an all-time high on Thursday as the U.S. Federal Reserve hinted at two possible interest rate cuts this year, bolstering bullion's appeal amid ongoing geopolitical and economic tensions.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

- - - -

Some of the main stories out in the South African press:

BUSINESS DAY

- State moves to bail out Amsa's long steel business

- Logistics woes threaten SA motor industry, says Ford boss

- Gwede Mantashe calls for more oil and gas drilling

data.title: Arcadis to Buy German Rail-focused WSP Infrastructure Engineering
data.description:
Arcadis said Thursday it agreed to buy Germany-based rail infrastructure company WSP Infrastructure Engineering.

The design and consultancy firm said the acquisition will double its presence in the German rail market, a key growth priority for Arcadis.

data.title: Prada Expands New York Flagship Store
data.description:
Prada's wholly owned subsidiary Prada USA signed a lease agreement for additional retail space at its flagship store on Fifth Avenue, New York, with an annual rent of $20 million.

The three-year lease, with a two-year renewal option, is scheduled to commence on June 1, 2025, with the rent increasing by 3% per year.

The expansion aims to strengthen Prada's presence in a prime retail location, according to a Wednesday filing with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The lessor, 720 Fifth USA, is an indirect subsidiary of Prada Holding, making the deal a connected transaction under Hong Kong's listing rules.

The agreement, signed on March 18, includes a rent-free period until the store opens.

data.title: France's Macron announces release of Olivier Grondeau from detention in Iran
data.description:
French citizen Olivier Grondeau was released from detention in Iran, French President Macron announced on Thursday in a post on X.

"We share his family's immense happiness and relief," said Macron.

Grondeau has spent 887 days in detention, said the country's Foreign Affairs Minister Jean-Noel Barrot in another post while also sharing a photo of the released Frenchman on a homecoming plane.

The Revolutionary Guards, elite corps of the Iranian army, have arrested dozens of dual nationals and foreigners in recent years, mainly on espionage and security charges.

Two other French citizens are still detained in Iran: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris, who were arrested in May 2022 during a tourist trip to Iran for alleged "espionage."

"Our mobilization will not weaken: Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris must be freed from Iranian jails," Macron said.

data.title: Scandinavian Astor Group focuses on stealth technology for military purposes - enters into an investment agreement in Dolprop Industries
data.description:
Scandinavian Astor Group AB

Scandinavian Astor Group AB (publ) (`Astor Group' or the `Company') announced today that the Company and its subsidiary Oscilion System AB have entered into an investment and licence agreement of SEK 15 million in the underwater technology company Dolprop Industries (`Dolprop'). The investment will be made with existing cash in Astor Group and is part of Astor Group's strategy to strengthen its position in the defence and security sector. The licence rights will be placed in Astor Group's subsidiary Oscilion System AB, which will change its name to Oscilion Naval Systems AB (`Oscilion Naval Systems') in connection with the transaction.

Under the terms of the investment agreement, the Company has undertaken to invest SEK 15 million and will receive a 15% ownership in Dolprop for this investment. The investment is conditional upon the signing of a shareholders' agreement by shareholders representing together with the Company at least 90 per cent of the number of shares in Dolprop after the investment. As of the date of this press release, shareholders representing more than 90 per cent have accepted the shareholders' agreement. Under the shareholders' agreement, the Company has received an option to increase its ownership in Dolprop to 51 per cent within a three-year period. Furthermore, the transaction is conditional upon the shareholders of Dolprop resolving to (i) approve the board of directors' resolution on a directed share issue to the Company and (ii) elect Mattias Hjorth to the board of directors of Dolprop. The transaction is also subject to approval under applicable foreign direct investment (FDI) rules.

Under the terms of the licence agreement, Oscilion Naval Systems obtains an exclusive right to use all of Dolprop's patents for the development of products for military and defence purposes. Upon actual sales of products based on the patents covered by the licence, Oscilion Naval Systems will, during the first ten years, pay a royalty of five percent of the actual sales. Thereafter, the royalty will decrease by half a per cent per year. Fredrik Bergman, current CEO of Oscilion and with a background from FMV T&E, will continue to lead the operations of Oscilion Naval Systems.

”We see great potential in Dolprop's technology for military stealth vessels and this investment is a strategic step for us to develop innovative solutions in underwater technology with stealth capabilities for the defence sector. The licence agreement gives us a unique opportunity to further develop Dolprop's technology while providing synergies for both Astor Group and Dolprop”, comments Mattias Hjorth, CEO of Astor Group.

More about Dolprop Industries

Dolprop Industries AB is a Swedish innovative company developing sustainable marine propulsion technology inspired by nature's evolution. The company has created a biomimetic (biomimcry) marine propulsion where a fin replaces the traditional propeller, providing superior efficiency, quiet operation and increased safety for both humans and animals. The technology enables marine propulsion with zero cavitation and minimal noise emissions.

Scandinavian Astor Group – Strength and security in all elements

For further information, please contact:

Scandinavian Astor Group's CEO Mattias Hjorth

E-mail: ir@astorgroup.se

This information is information that Scandinavian Astor Group AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation 596/2014. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person set out above, at the time specified by Astor Group's news distributor at the time of publication of this press release.

Please note that this is an English translation of a press release written in Swedish by Scandinavian Astor Group AB (publ), in the event of any inaccuracies, the Swedish version applies.

About Scandinavian Astor Group AB (publ)

Scandinavian Astor Group is active in the defence industry. The company is a supplier of products, services and related technical solutions in military defence and civil security as well as products for industries. Astor Group is a public company with shares traded under the ticker ASTOR on NGM Nordic SME and is dual listed on Boerse Stuttgart. The Company has a registered office in Stockholm, Sweden. Shark Communication AB, tel. +46 73-434 45 15, e-mail: info@sharkcom.se is the Company's Mentor. For more information about Astor Group's business, visit: www.astorgroup.se

data.title: Haowu Electromechanical to Build Crankshaft Production Line for 200 Million Yuan; Shares Soar 10%
data.description:
Sichuan Haowu Electromechanical plans to construct a crankshaft production line for 200 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

The plant will produce 600,000 units for new energy vehicles every year.

The construction and investment will be made through the Chinese vehicle parts manufacturer's subsidiary, Neijiang Jinhong Crankshaft.

The company's shares jumped 10% in recent trade.

data.title: RWE Raises Dividend After Earnings Declined But Beat Expectations
data.description:
By Pierre Bertrand

RWE raised its dividend after its 2024 earnings fell but beat analysts' expectations.

The German energy company said it achieved 2.32 billion euros ($2.53 billion) in adjusted net profit for last year, compared with 4.1 billion euros a year prior.

Adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization came to 5.68 billion euros, from 7.75 billion euros in 2023.

It compares with analysts' expectations of 2.22 billion euros in adjusted net profit from 5.5 billion euros in adjusted Ebitda, according to a company-provided consensus.

External revenue, meanwhile, declined on year to 24.22 billion euros, reflecting lower contributions from its offshore wind and flexible-generation divisions.

RWE said it would propose a 1.10 euros per share dividend for 2024, up 0.10 euros from 2023, adding that it intends to raise its dividend further to 1.20 euros for 2025.

RWE added that it expects that margins from electricity sales, will normalize this year. It also expects normalized earnings at its supply and trading segment.

The company said that for this year it is targeting between 1.3 billion euros and 1.8 billion euros in adjusted net profit, and is targeting between 4.55 billion and 5.15 billion euros in adjusted Ebitda.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: Sanofi to Acquire Autoimmune Disease Treatment From Dren Bio for Up to $1.9 Billion
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Sanofi said it would acquire Bispecific Myeloid Cell Engager, an autoimmune disease treatment, from Dren Bio, for up to $1.9 billion.

The French pharmaceutical company on Thursday said it would pay $600 million upfront and that the deal also includes potential future payments totaling $1.3 billion upon achievement of certain development and launch milestones. The transaction is expected to be financed with available cash resources, it said.

The deal seeks to broaden the company's immunology pipeline and is expected to close during the second quarter of 2025, it said.

"Deep B-cell depletion is at the frontier of treating autoimmune diseases and using the myeloid cell engager DR-0201 has the potential to elevate the treatment effect for patients, in particular patients refractory to existing treatments," Head of Research and Development Houman Ashrafian said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Hapag-Lloyd: Moderate Decline in Average Freight Rate is Anticipated
RocketBoots' Loss Prevention Trial Yields Positive ROI
data.description:
RocketBoots said its trial with a multinational retailer for its loss prevention software achieved the customer's objectives generating a positive return on investment, according to a Thursday Australian bourse filing.

The company said it also met the customer's strict data security requirements with respect to the General Data Protection Regulation, a critical condition required as a part of securing a contract.

Whilst the trial value at the start of the trial was considered immaterial, it is now material due to ongoing extensions and variations with the customer having paid fees equal to approximately 8% of the company's 2024 revenue, the filing added.

The company's shares were up 7% on market close.

data.title: Eli Lilly launches weight-loss drug Mounjaro in India after drug regulator approval
data.description: Eli Lilly has launched its blockbuster diabetes and weight-loss drug Mounjaro in India following approval from country's drug regulator, it said on Thursday.

data.title: Bridgepoint Group Target Raised to 410p From 390p by Citi
Bridgepoint Group Raised to Buy From Neutral by Citi
Babcock International to Rejoin FTSE 100 Index After Seven-Year Absence
data.description:
By Ian Walker

U.K. defense company Babcock International Group will rejoin the FTSE 100 index on Monday after a more than seven years of absence, FTSE Russell said late Wednesday.

Babcock--which is a major contractor for the U.K. government--will replace investment firm Hargreaves Lansdown, which is being taken over by a private-equity consortium in a 5.44 billion pounds ($7.07 billion) deal.

Babcock, which was last in the top index between June 2012 and December 2017, has seen its share price rocket 44% over the past 12 months as geopolitical tensions fueled demand for defense platforms and governments commit to increased spending.

On Feb. 6 the company raised its guidance for fiscal 2025 after a strong performance over its fiscal year to date.

Babcock shares closed at 755.50 pence on Wednesday implying a market capitalization of 3.82 billion pounds.

Hargreaves agreed to be bought by the consortium--which is made up of CVC Advisers, Nordic Capital and a subsidiary of the Abu Dhabi Investment Authority--last August. Shareholders approved the deal on Oct. 14 and the company will delist on March 24.

Write to Ian Walker at ian.walker@wsj.com

data.title: National Bank of Fujairah Resumes Trading on Abu Dhabi Bourse
data.description:
National Bank of Fujairah (ADX:NBF) recommenced trading on the Abu Dhabi Securities Exchange on Thursday, according to a same-day notice.

The trading suspension started Tuesday and was lifted after the United Arab Emirates-based lender published the results of its general assembly meeting.

data.title: RBC Downgrades Siemens as Valuation Shifts from Discount to Premium
data.description:
RBC Capital Markets lowered Siemens to sector perform from outperform, citing a more appropriate valuation premium after the stock's recent outperformance against the broader sector.

"We continue to like the business fundamentals, but with the stock close to our 245-euro price target, we downgrade to sector perform," RBC analysts wrote. "Siemens now trades at an 8% sector premium on EV/EBITA, having traded at an average ~8% sector discount since 2010."

RBC said the German technology group's ongoing review of its 75% stake in Siemens Healthineers has contributed to recent stock gains. While a decision is not expected until the company's December capital markets day, analysts noted that a divestment to just below 50% could release 15 billion euros, creating a balance sheet capacity of 34 billion euros.

Looking ahead, the research firm expects Siemens to generate 79.86 billion euros in revenue for fiscal 2025 and 85.01 billion euros for 2026. Net income is forecasted at 9.73 billion euros for 2025, declining to 8.51 billion euros for 2026.

data.title: NHPC Board Approves INR63 Billion Borrowing Plan for Fiscal 2025-26
data.description:
NHPC's board approved the proposed fundraising of up to 63 billion Indian rupees in fiscal 2025-2026, according to an Indian bourse filing on Wednesday.

The funds will be raised through secured or unsecured redeemable taxable non-convertible corporate bonds via private placement and/or term loans and external commercial borrowings in tranches, the filing said.

data.title: Boliden publishes Annual and Sustainability Report 2024
data.description:
Boliden

Boliden's Annual and Sustainability Report 2024 is now available at www.boliden.com. It highlights how Boliden creates value and covers topics such as strategy, operations, sustainability and financial performance. The report's overhead theme is A century of exploration and innovation.

Compared with previous annual reports, the Annual and Sustainability Report 2024 is adapted for digital reading with a landscape format and clickable navigation. A printed version, available in April, can only be ordered from Order Annual Report | Boliden.

The previously separately published Sustainability Index has been integrated into the sustainbility section of the Annual and Sustainability report 2024.

Boliden has also published the Annual and Sustainbility Report 2024 in Swedish in European Single Electronic Format (ESEF).

For further information, please contact:

Klas Nilsson

Director Group Communications

+46 70 453 65 88

Klas.Nilsson@boliden.com

This information is such that Boliden AB is obliged to make public pursuant to the Securities Markets Act. The information was submitted for publication at 07:45 CET on March 20, 2025.

Metals for generations to come

Boliden's vision is to be the most climate-friendly and respected metal provider in the world. We are Europe's producer of sustainable metals and, guided by our values care, courage and responsibility, we operate within exploration, mines, smelters and recycling. We are around 6,000 employees and have annual revenues of approximately SEK 90 billion. The share is listed in the Large Cap segment of NASDAQ Stockholm.

www.boliden.com

data.title: Alkhorayef Water & Power Technologies Bags SAR62 Million Leak Detection, Repair Contract
data.description:
Alkhorayef Water and Power Technologies (SASE:2081) secured a contract worth 61.8 million Saudi riyals from National Water Co., according to a Thursday release.

The water and wastewater management company will provide leak detection and repair services in Jazan, Saudi Arabia, for 30 months.
